title: While the Seat Rumours Swirl, Ferrari Have Already Been Speaking to a Former Champion Contender
meta: Published: 26 March 2026 | By Olly Bradshaw, Paddock Insider | F1CC News
caption: Ferrari Garage.
body:
With Ferrari’s driver lineup under growing scrutiny, it has emerged that the Scuderia have already been in direct contact with Liam Dela Cruz.

The German driver spent two seasons at the team before stepping away from Formula 1 earlier this year.

Sources close to the discussions say the contact was personal. It came from within the upper levels of the organisation.

Ferrari put a structured proposal to Dela Cruz in the weeks following his retirement. A proposal centred on his continued involvement with the team.

The arrangement was substantial, by any measure. Selected Grand Prix weekend appearances. Involvement in sponsor and media campaigns. Access to the factory simulator programme. A seat at the table for private Ferrari events and internal projects.

In the post‑career landscape of a Formula 1 driver, it would have been an attractive package. Ferrari are one of the most commercially powerful brands in the sport. The kind of continued access the role offered is not something teams routinely extend to departing drivers.

The implication was clear. They wanted Dela Cruz’s face, his reputation, his connection to the paddock, to remain part of the Scuderia’s broader world. Even if he was no longer behind the wheel.

He said no.

“He appreciated the offer, but he made it clear he wants to step away from Formula 1 completely for now,” one source familiar with the discussions said.

The rejection has reportedly surprised several figures across the paddock. Dela Cruz spent two seasons at Ferrari. Sixth in the 2024 championship with 158 points. Runner‑up in 2025 with 226. His relationship with the team was understood to be strong.

Many inside the sport had assumed Ferrari would be among the very few organisations he might remain connected to after stepping away. The speed and apparent firmness of the decline has changed that assumption.

Ferrari have not commented on the approach.

What is clear is that Dela Cruz, whose popularity with fans and standing in the paddock remained high at the point of his retirement, has opted for a clean break.

Whether that remains the case over the longer term…

Interesting.
